zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Champernowne於1278年出生在英國戴文莫德波利時，他的父親Sir Richard Champernoun23歲，母親Joan de Cornwall21歲。他和Elizabeth Valletort至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1338年11月26日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國戴文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Champernowne于1278年出生在英国戴文莫德波利时，他的父亲Sir Richard Champernoun23岁，母亲Joan de Cornwall21岁。他和Elizabeth Valletort至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1338年11月26日之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Champernowne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Cambernon in Manche.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Champernowne was born in 1278, in Modbury, Devon, England, his father, Sir Richard Champernoun, was 23 and his mother, Joan de Cornwall, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Elizabeth Valletort. He died before 26 November 1338, in his hometown, and was buried in Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Champernowne est né en 1278, à Modbury, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Champernoun, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Joan de Cornwall, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Elizabeth Valletort. Il est décédé avant 26 novembre 1338, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Champernowne 1278 in Modbury, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Champernoun 23 und seine Mutter Joan de Cornwall 21. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Valletort mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 26. November 1338 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Champernowne è nato il nel 1278 nel Modbury, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard Champernoun, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Cornwall, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Elizabeth Valletort. Morì prima del 26 novembre 1338, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Champernowneが1278年にイギリスデボンモドブリーで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Champernounは23歳，母Joan de Cornwallは21歳であった。彼はElizabeth Valletortとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1338年11月26日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Champernowne(이)가 1278년 영국 데폰 모드버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Champernoun(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Cornwall(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Valletort(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1338년 11월 26일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 데폰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Champernowne nasceu em 1278, em Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Champernoun, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Cornwall, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Elizabeth Valletort. Ele faleceu antes 26 de novembro de 1338, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Champernowne родился в 1278 г. в местности модбури, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Champernoun было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Cornwall, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Elizabeth Valletort. Он умер перед 26 ноябрь 1338 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Champernowne nació en 1278, en Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Champernoun, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Joan de Cornwall, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Elizabeth Valletort. Murió antes de 26 de noviembre de 1338, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Devon, Inglaterra.
